cases.rules_text:
1. Signal tampering includes swapping a blue signal tile, lighting a reverse tunnel lamp, inserting a red route key, or flipping a black signal lever.
2. Train rerouting is present when the ghost train changes track or the dispatch board repaints the route.
3. Route misdirection risk requires signal tampering together with train rerouting.
4. Seal breach requires moving a seal marker, entering the sealed track area, and the seal alarm ringing.
5. Dispatch authority requires a dispatch order, a matching signal-book entry, and confirmation from the control room.
6. Collapse avoidance requires a collapse indication, temporary lamp redirection, and a report to the control room.
7. A sensor fault condition requires a sensor fault report and a panel record showing no hand switch.
8. The unauthorized signal change basis is present when signal tampering occurs.
9. The route misdirection basis requires signal tampering together with train rerouting.
10. The sealed track breach basis requires breach of a sealed track condition and route-misdirection risk.
11. The dispatch order authorization basis requires dispatch authority connected to signal tampering.
12. The collapse avoidance authorization basis requires collapse-avoidance action connected to signal tampering.
13. The sensor fault exemption basis requires a sensor-fault condition connected to train rerouting.
14. The unauthorized signal change article covers unauthorized signal change basis; the prescribed consequence is signal reprimand.
15. The route misdirection article covers route misdirection basis; the prescribed consequence is route suspension.
16. The sealed track breach article covers sealed track breach basis; the prescribed consequence is tunnel access ban.
17. The dispatch order authorization article covers dispatch order authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
18. The collapse avoidance authorization article covers collapse avoidance authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
19. The sensor fault exemption article covers sensor fault exemption basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. Where the route misdirection article and the unauthorized signal change article both apply, the route misdirection article prevails.
21. Where the sealed track breach article and the unauthorized signal change article both apply, the sealed track breach article prevails.
22. Where the sealed track breach article and the route misdirection article both apply, the sealed track breach article prevails.
23. The dispatch order authorization article operates as an exception to the unauthorized signal change article when both apply.
24. The dispatch order authorization article operates as an exception to the route misdirection article when both apply.
25. The collapse avoidance authorization article operates as an exception to the unauthorized signal change article when both apply.
26. The collapse avoidance authorization article operates as an exception to the route misdirection article when both apply.
27. The collapse avoidance authorization article operates as an exception to the sealed track breach article when both apply.
28. The sensor fault exemption article operates as an exception to the route misdirection article when both apply.
cases.case_text: Alex swapped a blue signal tile in the lower tunnel. The ghost train did not change track. The dispatch board did not repaint the train route. The seal alarm did not ring. Alex did not hold a dispatch order. No ceiling collapse was detected near the track. Ground sonar did not mark a collapse pocket near the track. The tunnel sensor did not report a fault.

cases.rules_text:
1. Signal tampering includes swapping a blue signal tile, lighting a reverse tunnel lamp, inserting a red route key, or flipping a black signal lever.
2. Train rerouting is present when the ghost train changes track or the dispatch board repaints the route.
3. Route misdirection risk requires signal tampering together with train rerouting.
4. Seal breach requires moving a seal marker, entering the sealed track area, and the seal alarm ringing.
5. Dispatch authority requires a dispatch order, a matching signal-book entry, and confirmation from the control room.
6. Collapse avoidance requires a collapse indication, temporary lamp redirection, and a report to the control room.
7. A sensor fault condition requires a sensor fault report and a panel record showing no hand switch.
8. The unauthorized signal change basis is present when signal tampering occurs.
9. The route misdirection basis requires signal tampering together with train rerouting.
10. The sealed track breach basis requires breach of a sealed track condition and route-misdirection risk.
11. The dispatch order authorization basis requires dispatch authority connected to signal tampering.
12. The collapse avoidance authorization basis requires collapse-avoidance action connected to signal tampering.
13. The sensor fault exemption basis requires a sensor-fault condition connected to train rerouting.
14. The unauthorized signal change article covers unauthorized signal change basis; the prescribed consequence is signal reprimand.
15. The route misdirection article covers route misdirection basis; the prescribed consequence is route suspension.
16. The sealed track breach article covers sealed track breach basis; the prescribed consequence is tunnel access ban.
17. The dispatch order authorization article covers dispatch order authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
18. The collapse avoidance authorization article covers collapse avoidance authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
19. The sensor fault exemption article covers sensor fault exemption basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. Where the route misdirection article and the unauthorized signal change article both apply, the route misdirection article prevails.
21. Where the sealed track breach article and the unauthorized signal change article both apply, the sealed track breach article prevails.
22. Where the sealed track breach article and the route misdirection article both apply, the sealed track breach article prevails.
23. The dispatch order authorization article operates as an exception to the unauthorized signal change article when both apply.
24. The dispatch order authorization article operates as an exception to the route misdirection article when both apply.
25. The collapse avoidance authorization article operates as an exception to the unauthorized signal change article when both apply.
26. The collapse avoidance authorization article operates as an exception to the route misdirection article when both apply.
27. The collapse avoidance authorization article operates as an exception to the sealed track breach article when both apply.
28. The sensor fault exemption article operates as an exception to the route misdirection article when both apply.

cases.rules_text:
1. Signal tampering includes swapping a blue signal tile, lighting a reverse tunnel lamp, inserting a red route key, or flipping a black signal lever.
2. Train rerouting is present when the ghost train changes track or the dispatch board repaints the route.
3. Route misdirection risk requires signal tampering together with train rerouting.
4. Seal breach requires moving a seal marker, entering the sealed track area, and the seal alarm ringing.
5. Dispatch authority requires a dispatch order, a matching signal-book entry, and confirmation from the control room.
6. Collapse avoidance requires a collapse indication, temporary lamp redirection, and a report to the control room.
7. A sensor fault condition requires a sensor fault report and a panel record showing no hand switch.
8. The unauthorized signal change basis is present when signal tampering occurs.
9. The route misdirection basis requires signal tampering together with train rerouting.
10. The sealed track breach basis requires breach of a sealed track condition and route-misdirection risk.
11. The dispatch order authorization basis requires dispatch authority connected to signal tampering.
12. The collapse avoidance authorization basis requires collapse-avoidance action connected to signal tampering.
13. The sensor fault exemption basis requires a sensor-fault condition connected to train rerouting.

15. The route misdirection article covers route misdirection basis; the prescribed consequence is route suspension.

cases.case_text: Alex swapped a blue signal tile in the lower tunnel. The ghost train changed track. Alex moved a seal marker from the track gate. Alex entered the sealed track area. The seal alarm rang. Alex held a dispatch order. Alex logged the order in the signal book. The control room confirmed the order. A ceiling collapse was detected near the track. Alex redirected the tunnel lamp temporarily. Alex reported the change to the control room. The dispatch board did not repaint the train route. Ground sonar did not mark a collapse pocket near the track. The tunnel sensor did not report a fault.

cases.rules_text:
1. Signal tampering includes swapping a blue signal tile, lighting a reverse tunnel lamp, inserting a red route key, or flipping a black signal lever.
2. Train rerouting is present when the ghost train changes track or the dispatch board repaints the route.
3. Route misdirection risk requires signal tampering together with train rerouting.
4. Seal breach requires moving a seal marker, entering the sealed track area, and the seal alarm ringing.
5. Dispatch authority requires a dispatch order, a matching signal-book entry, and confirmation from the control room.
6. Collapse avoidance requires a collapse indication, temporary lamp redirection, and a report to the control room.
7. A sensor fault condition requires a sensor fault report and a panel record showing no hand switch.
8. The unauthorized signal change basis is present when signal tampering occurs.
9. The route misdirection basis requires signal tampering together with train rerouting.
10. The sealed track breach basis requires breach of a sealed track condition and route-misdirection risk.
11. The dispatch order authorization basis requires dispatch authority connected to signal tampering.
12. The collapse avoidance authorization basis requires collapse-avoidance action connected to signal tampering.
13. The sensor fault exemption basis requires a sensor-fault condition connected to train rerouting.
14. The unauthorized signal change article covers unauthorized signal change basis; the prescribed consequence is signal reprimand.
15. The route misdirection article covers route misdirection basis; the prescribed consequence is route suspension.
16. The sealed track breach article covers sealed track breach basis; the prescribed consequence is tunnel access ban.
17. The dispatch order authorization article covers dispatch order authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
18. The collapse avoidance authorization article covers collapse avoidance authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
19. The sensor fault exemption article covers sensor fault exemption basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. Where the route misdirection article and the unauthorized signal change article both apply, the route misdirection article prevails.
21. Where the sealed track breach article and the unauthorized signal change article both apply, the sealed track breach article prevails.
22. Where the sealed track breach article and the route misdirection article both apply, the sealed track breach article prevails.
23. The dispatch order authorization article operates as an exception to the unauthorized signal change article when both apply.
24. The dispatch order authorization article operates as an exception to the route misdirection article when both apply.
25. The collapse avoidance authorization article operates as an exception to the unauthorized signal change article when both apply.
26. The collapse avoidance authorization article operates as an exception to the route misdirection article when both apply.
27. The collapse avoidance authorization article operates as an exception to the sealed track breach article when both apply.
28. The sensor fault exemption article operates as an exception to the route misdirection article when both apply.
cases.case_text: Hayden inserted a red route key into the signal panel. The ghost train changed track. Hayden held a dispatch order. Hayden logged the order in the signal book. The control room confirmed the order. A ceiling collapse was detected near the track. Hayden redirected the tunnel lamp temporarily. Hayden reported the change to the control room. The tunnel sensor reported a fault. The panel record showed no hand switch. The dispatch board did not repaint the train route. The seal alarm did not ring. Ground sonar did not mark a collapse pocket near the track.

cases.rules_text:
1. Signal tampering includes swapping a blue signal tile, lighting a reverse tunnel lamp, inserting a red route key, or flipping a black signal lever.
2. Train rerouting is present when the ghost train changes track or the dispatch board repaints the route.
3. Route misdirection risk requires signal tampering together with train rerouting.
4. Seal breach requires moving a seal marker, entering the sealed track area, and the seal alarm ringing.
5. Dispatch authority requires a dispatch order, a matching signal-book entry, and confirmation from the control room.
6. Collapse avoidance requires a collapse indication, temporary lamp redirection, and a report to the control room.
7. A sensor fault condition requires a sensor fault report and a panel record showing no hand switch.
8. The unauthorized signal change basis is present when signal tampering occurs.
9. The route misdirection basis requires signal tampering together with train rerouting.
10. The sealed track breach basis requires breach of a sealed track condition and route-misdirection risk.
11. The dispatch order authorization basis requires dispatch authority connected to signal tampering.
12. The collapse avoidance authorization basis requires collapse-avoidance action connected to signal tampering.
13. The sensor fault exemption basis requires a sensor-fault condition connected to train rerouting.
14. The unauthorized signal change article covers unauthorized signal change basis; the prescribed consequence is signal reprimand.
15. The route misdirection article covers route misdirection basis; the prescribed consequence is route suspension.
16. The sealed track breach article covers sealed track breach basis; the prescribed consequence is tunnel access ban.
17. The dispatch order authorization article covers dispatch order authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
18. The collapse avoidance authorization article covers collapse avoidance authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
19. The sensor fault exemption article covers sensor fault exemption basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
20. Where the route misdirection article and the unauthorized signal change article both apply, the route misdirection article prevails.
21. Where the sealed track breach article and the unauthorized signal change article both apply, the sealed track breach article prevails.
22. Where the sealed track breach article and the route misdirection article both apply, the sealed track breach article prevails.
23. The dispatch order authorization article operates as an exception to the unauthorized signal change article when both apply.
24. The dispatch order authorization article operates as an exception to the route misdirection article when both apply.
25. The collapse avoidance authorization article operates as an exception to the unauthorized signal change article when both apply.
26. The collapse avoidance authorization article operates as an exception to the route misdirection article when both apply.
27. The collapse avoidance authorization article operates as an exception to the sealed track breach article when both apply.
28. The sensor fault exemption article operates as an exception to the route misdirection article when both apply.
cases.case_text: Reese lit a reverse tunnel lamp. The dispatch board repainted the train route. Ground sonar marked a collapse pocket near the track. Reese redirected the tunnel lamp temporarily. Reese reported the change to the control room. The tunnel sensor reported a fault. The panel record showed no hand switch. The ghost train did not change track. The seal alarm did not ring. Reese did not hold a dispatch order. No ceiling collapse was detected near the track.
